lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, the entire gang is there, eating breakfast. Phoebe is on the couch, fidgeting.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What am I sitting on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Top of the world? Dock of the bay? (He tries to think of another but can't) I'm out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (taking something out of the couch) Ew-eww!! Undies!
She throws them into the kitchen and Rachel picks them up with the handle of a large spoon. Chandler and Monica have horrified looks on their faces.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right! Who's are they? Who's are they?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, they're not mine!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, they're Joey's! They gotta be Joey's!
Rachel turns and stares at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, they're mine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? They're Joey's! J-J-J-J-J-Joey's!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why are they here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know uhh... (Pause as he thinks about it.) Well, I'm Joey. Yeah, I'm disgusting, I take my underwear off in other people's homes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, get 'em out of here! What's wrong with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (waving them in his face) Take 'em! (Joey makes a noise and jumps out of the way.) Joey, you can touch them! They're your underwear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (reluctantly taking them) Chandler? A word.
Follows Joey into their apartment and shrugs on his way out.
[Cut to the guy's apartment.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's it! I'm tired of covering for you two! This has got to stop! (Realizes he still has the underwear in his hand.) Ahh! (Throws them towards Chandler's room.) And tighty-whiteys! What are you, 8?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Thank you Joey, thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, no, you're not welcome. Okay, look, I hate this! You guys keep embarrassing me! (To Monica) Yesterday, Rachel found your razor in our bathroom and I didn't know what to say, so I said it was mine and-and that I was playing a woman in a play. And one thing led to another and (He puts his leg on the chair and pulls up his pants leg to reveal that he now has shaved legs.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (inspecting his leg) Wow! And around the ankles, y'know that is a tough spot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it was! All right, listen, I can't...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting him) All this lying has been hard on us too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh, yeah-yeah, I bet all the sex makes it easier!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, yeah actually.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'll try to be more careful okay? It's just that, we don't want everyone to know because this is going really well, and maybe the reason it's going really well is because it's a secret.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know it sounds really weird, but we're just so bad at relationships.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We are! Help us!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Help!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! But, (To Monica) you do it with me once.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Didn't think so.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey, Chandler, Monica, and Rachel are there as Phoebe enters with her nose stuck in a book.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you reading?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, Wethering Heights. I'm taking a literature class at the New School and I have to finish it for the first session tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know you were taking a class. That is so cool.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Well, I really liked that Lamaze class I took! Y'know and this time I thought I'd go for something, y'know a little more intellectual, with a less painful final exam.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey that sounds like fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Ooh, you should come with me! Oh yeah, then I'd have someone to sit with!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Okay-ooh, but are you going to have time to read it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I read that in high school.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is going to be so much fun! Okay-shhh, I have to finish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, depressed) Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's wrong buddy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Someone at work ate my sandwich!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what did the police say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My Thanksgiving leftover sandwich. I can't believe someone ate it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, it's just a sandwich!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just a sandwich? Look, I am 30 years old, I'm about to be divorced twice and I just got evicted! That sandwich was the only good thing going on in my life! Someone ate the only good thing going on in my life!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, look, I-I have enough stuff for one more sandwich, I mean I was going to eat it myself, but (motions that he can have it.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (quietly) That-that would be incredible. Thank you so much. I-I still can't believe someone ate it!! I mean, look, I left a note and everything.
Shows the note to Chandler who reads it aloud.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading) Knock-knock. Who's there? Ross Geller's lunch. Ross Geller's lunch, who? Ross Geller's lunch, please don't take me. Okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm surprised you didn't go home wearing your lunch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, look you wanna hold onto your food? You gotta scare people off. I learned that living on the street.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! So what would you say Pheebs? Stuff like uh, "Keep your mitts off my grub?"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Say Ross, when you picture Phoebe living on the street, is she surrounded by the entire cast of Annie?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this will keep them away from your stuff. (Writes him a note and the gang reads it.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Whoa! Ohh!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you are a bad ass!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Someday I'll, tell you about the time I stabbed the cop.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he stabbed me first!!
Phoebe's class, the class has already started and Rachel walks in late.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Sorry I'm late, but I left late.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So Pheebs, what is the book about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I thought you said you read it in high school.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, but then I remembered I started it and there was this pep rally and I was, I was on top of the pyramid but anyway-umm, what is this book about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, umm, it's this tragic love story between Cathy and Heathcliff and umm, it takes place on like these really creepy mores in England. Which I think represents the wildness of Heathcliff's character. I totally get symbolism.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: How would you characterize the theme of this book, uh let's see here (looks at his attendance sheet), Rachel Green?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, well I would have to say that it's a, it's tragic love story.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Well, that's sort of a given, but yes. Anyone else?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh-oh, symbolism! And uh, the-the uh, wildness of the mores, which I think is-is mirrored in the wildness of Heathcliff's character.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Excellent! What Rachel has shrewdly observed here...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) You completely stole my answer!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, honey that was pretty obvious.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well how would you know?! You didn't even read it!

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: What do you think? You in the blue shirt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think that uh, yours is a question with many answers.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Would ya care to venture one?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Would you care to venture one?

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Are you just repeating what I'm saying?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you just repeating what I'm saying?

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: All right, let's move on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay then.
Central Perk, Monica is there as Phoebe and Rachel return from the class.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah but why didn't you just say that you didn't read the book?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Be-because I didn't want him to think I was stupid! I mean, that was really embarrassing what happened to you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe! You're note, amazing! Not only did no one touch my sandwich, but people at work are actually afraid of me. Yeah, a guy called me mental! Mental Geller, yeah, I always wanted a cool nickname like that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, the best you got in high school was Wet Pants Geller.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was the water fountain! Okay?! Anyway, people are writing reports for me, uh pushing back deadlines to meet my schedule, I'm telling you, you get tough with people you can get anything you want. (Joey walks by with a cup of coffee.) Hey Tribbiani, give me that coffee! Now!
Joey casually pushes Ross over the back of the couch and sits down proud of himself.
The hallway, Joey is returning from a date with Cynthia.

lines.speaker: Cynthia
lines.text: God, this was really fun! I've been wondering if you were going to ask me out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you uh, still wondering?

lines.speaker: Cynthia
lines.text: No, we just went out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're smart. I like that.

He goes to open the door to his apartment, but finds it locked. As he's getting out his keys, Chandler and Monica quickly jump up from making out in the living room and run to Chandler's bedroom. The apartment has about 20 candles burning all over the place. Joey opens the door and ushers Cynthia in.

lines.speaker: Cynthia
lines.text: Oh, candles! (Notices something.) What is that? A blanket? A video camera? Oh my God! (As she storms out, Rachel returns and overhears the conversation.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no, wait-wait-wait!!

lines.speaker: Cynthia
lines.text: I can't believe you thought that you were going to video tape us having sex on the first date! (She storms away and Rachel enters to confront Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hiya.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, is what she just said umm-Oh my God. (Looks around the room.) You were actually gonna... (Chandler picks this moment to return to the living room.) (Rachel stares in shock.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is going on here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And with Chandler in the next room. What are you, what are you sick?
Chandler silently pleads with Joey to cover for them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm Joey. I mean, I'm disgusting. I make low-budget adult films. (Points at Chandler, angrily.)
Commercial Break
Chandler, Joey, and Ross's; continued from earlier. Joey is closing the door after Rachel leaves and is about to confront Chandler and Monica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys promised you'd be more careful! I mean, come on! The good Joey name is being dragged through the mud here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm telling everyone about you! That's the only way to explain the underwear and the video camera that doesn't make me look like a pig!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no, wait! There's got to be a better explanation. You can tell them you had to make an adult film for your (Thinks) adult film class.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I like that. But no-no, how does that explain why Rachel found my underwear at your place?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-I don't know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, get ready to come out of the non-gay closet!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just wait, please. I promise we'll come up with something. Just give us a little more time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Hey, but it better make me look really, really good. (Starts for his room.) Oh, and another thing, the video camera? Nice!!
The Museum of Prehistoric History (Ross's work); Ross is in the break room eating lunch as his boss, Dr. Leedbetter walks in.

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Umm, Ross. May I have a word with you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, of course, Donald.

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: We've been getting reports of some very angry behavior on your part.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Threatening letters, refusal to meet deadlines, apparently people now call you mental.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Proudly) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: We want you to speak to a psychiatrist.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no, you-you don't understand. Ugh, this is so silly. Umm, this is all because of a sandwich.

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: (laughs) A sandwich?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. You see my-my sister makes these amazing turkey sandwiches. Her secret is, she puts a, an extra slice of gravy soaked bread in the middle; I call it the Moist Maker. Anyway, I-I put my sandwich in the fridge over here...

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: (laughs) Oh, you know what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: I-I'm sorry. I, I-I-I believe I ate that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You ate my sandwich?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: It was a simple mistake. It could happen to anyone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (getting upset) Oh-oh really? Did you confuse it with your own turkey sandwich with a Moist Maker?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you perhaps seeing a note on top of it?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: There may have been a-a joke or a limerick of some kind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (getting angry) That said it was my sandwich?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Now-now calm down. Come look in my office, some of it my still be in the trash.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (jumping to his feet in anger) What?

lines.speaker: Dr. Leedbetter
lines.text: Well, it was quite large. I-I-I-I-I had to throw most of it away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You-you-you-you (trying to remain in control) threw my sandwich away!
[Cut to an outside shot of the museum.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (losing control, we hear him shout outside) MY SANDWICH?!!!
[Cut to a shot of a park.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: MY SANDWICH!!!!!! (Ross's scream scares a flight of pigeons away.)
Phoebe and Rachel's class; Rachel walks in, on time this time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sitting down) So umm, what's this book about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn't read this one either?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I was gonna, but I accidentally read something else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Vogue! Hey, so tell me about this Jane Eyre woman.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! You should've read it yourself!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on Phoebe! Don't be such a goodie-goodie!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! Okay, all right, so Jane Eyre, first of all, you'd think she's a woman, but she's not. She's a cyborg.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A cyborg?! Isn't that like a robot?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, this book was light years ahead of its time.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: (entering) Sorry I'm late. Let's get started. So, what did everybody think about Jane Eyre?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, Rachel and I were just discussing it and she had some very interesting insights.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Well, go ahead Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, thank you Phoebe. Umm, well, what struck me most when reading Jane Eyre was uh, how the book was so ahead of its time.

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: If you're talking about feminism, I think you're right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, feminism yes, but also the robots.
Central Perk, Chandler is on the couch as Monica joins him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. Okay, so umm, since that video camera thing didn't work out uh, I thought that I would give you just a little preview. (Hands him a Polaroid.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (gasps) You're naked in this picture!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.
Ross walks in, eating cotton candy. Monica nudges Chandler who hides the picture in his magazine. Ross sits down on the chair, he seems kinda out of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (in a stupor) Hey Chandler. (Sees Monica.) Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm fine! I saw a psychiatrist at work today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: On account of my rage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which I may say, right now, is out of control.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He gave me a pill for it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A pill?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh. Well, when the psychiatrist told me I had to take a leave of absence because I yelled at my boss I started to get worked up again, so he offered me a tranquilizer. And I thought was a good idea so, I took it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, they're making you take time off work?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you're okay with that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know. It's going to be weird not having a job for a while, but I, I definitely don't care about my sandwich.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Phoebe are returning from class.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, angrily) Ugh, that was so embarrassing! I can't believe you let me go on and on like that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiling) I'm sorry. It was just so funny when you started comparing Jane Eyre to Robocop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was not funny!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I snapped! Okay? You weren't taking the class seriously.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, come on! What is the big deal? I thought this was going to be something we could do together! Y'know, I thought it would be fun!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, yeah! Fun is good, but y'know I also wanted to learn. Y'know, people are always talking about what they learned in high school and I never went to high school.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh. Oh, so you really wanted to learn. Yeah, y'know, Pheebs I just wanted to have fun. Ohh, you know who you should go with?
The Class; Monica has taken Rachel's spot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (yelling and waving her hand in the air) I know! I know! I know!

lines.speaker: The Teacher
lines.text: Monica, you asked the question.
She sits back defeated, and Phoebe groans with disgust.

Chandler, Joey, and Ross's; Joey returns carrying a bucket of chicken, and starts going through the mail. While doing this, Monica's picture falls out. He bends over to pick it up and gasps. While he's staring at the picture, Rachel decides to come over and sees him looking at the picture.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sees the picture) Oh my God! That's Monica!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no-no! No-no-no-no-no-no-no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You get away from me!! You sick, sick, sick, sick-o!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, with the rest of the gang) What's going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey has got a secret peephole!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Sees the picture) Oh no! No! No! No! (Monica gasps as well.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! He has a naked picture of Monica! He takes naked pictures of us! And then he eats chicken and looks at them!
Ross stares in shock at him as he angrily puts down the chicken and takes off his coat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look! (Shows Ross the picture.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (covering his eyes) Dude! That's my sister! (She shows the rest of the gang.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (grabbing the picture) Give me that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, wait! Just wait. Everybody just calm down. Okay? Let's give our friend Joey a chance to explain why he's such a big pervert!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! I am not a pervert! Okay? It's just... I just... Kinda...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, look! Look. I think I can explain this.
He walks over and stands behind Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey's a sex addict.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!! (He turns around and stares at Chandler who's silently pleading with Joey to go along with it.) No I'm not!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay! It's good! It's good. It's a disease!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No! I am not a sex addict!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes you are! That's the only way to explain all this stuff!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No it isn't! No, it's not. Because you can also explain it with the truth!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what is the truth?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (thinking) I slept with Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well let's....let's see what everybody thinks of that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You slept with my sister?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh yes, but it was, we just did it once uh, in London.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is not good for my rage. (Takes another pill.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, is this true?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course it's true! How else would you explain all the weird stuff that's been going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes it's true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, but if it only happened that one time, how come we found your underwear in our apartment the other day?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh-oy! That was the underwear I was wearing that night in London. Right Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess I wanted to keep it (Pause) as a souvenir.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My God Monica!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you sure Joe? Are you sure you're not just a sex addict?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! If anyone's a sex addict here, it's Monica! Yeah. Yeah. She has been trying to get me back in the sack ever since London!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So that's why she gave you a naked picture of herself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That makes sense!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And the video camera?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess I set up the video camera to try and entice Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But sadly I could not be enticed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Unbelievable! I mean you really kept Joey's underwear?! Why? Why would you do that?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm Monica. I'm disgusting. I stalk guys and keep their underpants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I think we've all learned something about who's disgusting and who's not. Eh? All right, now, I'm going to get back to my bucket. I'm only eating the skin, so the chicken's up for grabs. (Offers it to everyone.)
Ending Credits
Monica and Phoebe's class; the class has ended and Phoebe is talking to one of her classmates.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I really thought you making a good point. I mean y'know, until you got cut off.

lines.speaker: A Female Student
lines.text: Yeah, what's up with that girl Monica?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know! I didn't come with her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering, happily) All right everybody! Everybody guess what? I just convinced Paul to give us a test next week!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: A test?!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Tests make us all better learners! Oh yeah! (Running out) We should have essay questions!!
End
